A model of '''mining droid''' was used to mine [[Keschel]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tyne's Horky]].

==History==
[[File:Mining_Droid_blasting_Mon_Julpa.jpg||left|thumb|200px|A mining droid firing at [[Mon Julpa]].]]
[[Putch Gundarian]] needed these [[Droid/Legends|droids]] in [[Gender/Legends|his]] [[Keschel]] [[Mining/Legends|mine]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tyne's Horky]]. He sent his nephew, [[Jann Tosh]], to buy one of them. Jann later exchanged the [[Mining droid/Legends|mining droid]] for [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]], [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], and an anonymous [[Android/Legends|android]] to save them from [[Yorpo Mog]]. Gundy was upset to learn that Jann had traded his [[Labor droid/Legends|work droid]] once they returned to his camp.

The [[Dirconite]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Kleb Zellock]] owned multiple mining droids of this series. He mostly used them to either guard or work his [[Nergon-14/Legends|Nergon-14]] mine. All of his droids were destroyed when his mine over-loaded and self-destructed.

Several of these mining droids were also present on the planet [[Biitu]] as captives of the [[Great Heep]].

==Behind the scenes==
These mining droids had a much different appearance in the art of ''[[The Lost Prince: A Droid Adventure]]'', featuring bronze colored bodies and bullet shaped heads.
